behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real `git` subprocess + CLI parsing/aggregation. A common real failure is invoking diff/status without rename detection or misreading real rename status codes, which only shows up against an actual repository state. Considered Integration, but rejected because behavior text is end-to-end CLI output for real repository changes, not just parser logic in isolation.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against that repo, and assert report shows exactly one rename and no corresponding add/delete inflation for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git diff behavior for binary blobs and real subprocess/stdout decoding behavior. Mocked diff text can miss real binary edge cases (encoding, markers, exit behavior) and hide crashes. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real git repo with at least one binary file change, run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and the report includes the binary file in the correct count/category.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure arises from real git behavior on unborn HEAD/no-commit repos (often non-zero exit or special refs), which is subprocess/OS boundary behavior. Considered Integration, but rejected because behavior explicitly targets repository state that triggers real git command semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI, and assert successful exit plus an empty structured report to stdout instead of an exception/error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end correctness claim against actual repository history, requiring real git history traversal and subprocess outputs. In-process stubs can validate arithmetic but can hide mismatches caused by real command selection/range handling/pathspec behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing known numbers of adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run the CLI on that repo, and assert reported totals match the ground-truth history you created.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged is git index/working-tree semantics, which is real repository + subprocess behavior. Mocking can miss index-specific edge cases and command flag differences. Considered Integration, but rejected because behavior text explicitly compares real git states (staged/unstaged).
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, produce equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI in both states, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout output.
